rows.notes: Clear page title at top, fully legible handwriting and no amounts in same line. Vertical ruling lines not involved for this row.

rows.description: Mr Skelton. Portrait of Bishop of Montreal
rows.notes: Text clearly readable. Pound/shilling/pence columns unambiguous; amounts read as 2 2 0 against the vertical column lines.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Skelton for painting/portrait of the Bishop of Montreal for the college collection.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Skelton for a portrait of the Bishop of Montreal, amounting to £2 2s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 20 0 0 is large and clearly aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Description legible as 'Gratuity to Jackson'.
rows.english_description: A gratuity or gift paid to Jackson.
rows.english_description_with_amount: A gratuity paid to Jackson, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.description: Mr Skelton Bill for altering ceiling
rows.notes: Description legible; amount 18 2 0 clearly aligned. 'Bill' spelled with a single l in the manuscript but reading is unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Payment of Mr Skelton's bill for altering a ceiling (building alteration work).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Mr Skelton's bill for altering a ceiling, amounting to £18 2s 0d.

rows.description: Bill for thatching Cottage at Campsfield
rows.notes: Description readable; numeric columns indicate 1 19 7. Pence and shillings fit valid ranges. Minor uncertainty only in the place name 'Campsfield' spelling.
rows.english_description: Payment for thatching work carried out on the cottage at Campsfield.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for thatching the cottage at Campsfield, amounting to £1 19s 7d.

rows.description: Margetts proportion of his bill for Tablets for the Chancel at Kidlington - by Vote of College
rows.notes: Long descriptive line but amount (20 0 0) is clearly written and aligned. Several words in description are faint/abbreviated ('Margetts' and 'College') so description text chosen to match best reading of the script.
rows.english_description: College-paid share of Margetts's bill for tablets (memorial/inscription tablets) in the chancel at Kidlington, approved by college vote.
rows.english_description_with_amount: College-paid share of Margetts's bill for tablets for the Kidlington chancel, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.description: Journey to L. Newington & L. Tw on Coll. business
rows.notes: Amount 1 13 6 aligns clearly. Description is somewhat abbreviated in the manuscript ('L. Newington & L. Tw'); transcription follows the visible abbreviations, but the exact place names/abbreviations are slightly uncertain.
rows.english_description: Travel expenses for a journey on college business to Little Newington and L. Tw.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Travel expenses for a journey to Little Newington and L. Tw on college business, amounting to £1 13s 6d.

rows.description: Allowed Mr Harris, Little Jew, Bills
rows.notes: This line appears as a label/header for a grouped set of sub-items (curly brace in manuscript). No amount written on this line itself. Handwriting for 'Little Jew' is ambiguous; chosen transcription follows visible letters but may be slightly off.
rows.english_description: Allowance made for bills to Mr Harris and to 'Little Jew' (items approved for payment).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Allowance made for bills to Mr Harris and to 'Little Jew'; amount not stated.

rows.notes: Amount reads 2 2 6 clearly in the columns. 'Beck's Cottage' legible; small uncertainty only from ink thickness but numeric alignment is clear.
rows.english_description: Payments for repairs carried out at Beck's Cottage.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payments for repairs to Beck's Cottage, amounting to £2 2s 6d.

rows.notes: Short description, very clear handwriting; amount 2 0 0 well aligned with ruling lines.
rows.english_description: Payment for a new stable door (repair/replacement of stable fittings).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for a new stable door, amounting to £2 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 8 16 10 is clear in the currency columns. The item word looks like 'Rindler' or 'Ridder' (handwriting ambiguous); chosen reading matches letter shapes but uncertain by one or two letters.
rows.english_description: Purchase of an iron rindler (garden tool or implement) for the college garden.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Purchase of an iron rindler for the garden, amounting to £8 16s 10d.

rows.description: Mr Harris & Mr Ambers share of dinner at the Pupils
rows.notes: Amount 3 5 0 visible. Description is partially obscured/faint; the recipient names and the venue word are uncertain ('Ambers' and 'Pupils' are best-effort readings).
rows.english_description: Payment of the shared cost of a dinner involving Mr Harris and Mr Ambers for the pupils.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Share of the dinner for Mr Harris and Mr Ambers at the pupils, amounting to £3 5s 0d.

rows.notes: Manuscript shows a grouped subtotal (aligned right) of 16 4 4 for the brace of items under 'Allowed Mr Harris...'. No explicit text label on same line; amount alignment and grouping mark make this a subtotal.

rows.description: Timble. Bills at Merton - Stone Slater
rows.notes: Amount 2 15 8 clearly in columns. Description partly abbreviated ('Timble' and 'Stone Slater') but legible in context.
rows.english_description: Bills for stone-slating work at Merton (reference 'Timble' noted alongside the accounts).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Bills for stone-slating work at Merton, amounting to £2 15s 8d.

rows.notes: Amount reads as 0 8 9 (no pounds written, shillings 8 pence 9). The trade names are clear; pounds column empty which I record as 0.
rows.english_description: Payment to a joiner/carpenter for carpentry work.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to a joiner/carpenter, amounting to £0 8s 9d.

rows.notes: A subtotal 9 4 5 appears aligned to the right for the Timble/Merton bill group. No explicit text on this line; grouping mark indicates total.
Amount 1 5 10 legibly aligned. Description minimal but clear; 'drawing' word is clearly the item for the printer allowance.
rows.english_description: An allowance paid to the printer for drawing/illustration work.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Allowance to the printer for drawing, amounting to £1 5s 10d.

rows.notes: Very clear large loan entry 100 0 0; numbers and name 'Mr Rutherford' are legible. Alignment is unambiguous.
rows.english_description: A loan advanced to Mr Rutherford.
rows.english_description_with_amount: A loan advanced to Mr Rutherford, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 1 0 0 clearly visible and aligned. Description reads 'Receipt Stamps' though first word could be singular/plural; context indicates stamps purchased.
rows.english_description: Purchase of receipt stamps (stationery/office expense).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Purchase of receipt stamps, amounting to £1 0s 0d.

rows.description: Mr Campbell - subscription to Circuit for L. New
rows.notes: Amount 15 0 0 is clear. Description has several abbreviations/uncertain words in the manuscript ('Circuit' and 'L. New' are best-effort readings), so transcription of description is somewhat uncertain.
rows.english_description: Subscription paid by Mr Campbell towards the circuit (likely a local circuit/account) for L. New.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Subscription by Mr Campbell to the circuit for L. New, amounting to £15 0s 0d.

rows.description: Low, Gardener, one load of coals
rows.notes: Amount 1 6 0 clear. Name 'Low' (gardener) and 'one load of coals' readable though small; alignment confirms numbers.
rows.english_description: Payment to the gardener Low for one load of coals.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Low, the gardener, for one load of coals, amounting to £1 6s 0d.

rows.description: Davies, Mounding Almanack for Lord Peter
rows.notes: Amount 1 10 0 visible. The description is faint/abbreviated — 'Mounding Almanack for Lord Peter' is my best reading of the script but could be slightly different; numbers are clear.
rows.english_description: Payment to Davies for providing/mounting an almanack for Lord Peter (gift or presentation item).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Davies for mounting an almanack for Lord Peter, amounting to £1 10s 0d.

rows.description: Grove, Bills for Venison Junr 30th last
rows.notes: Amount 5 17 0 is clearly aligned. Description indicates 'Bills for Venison' with date; 'Junr' reading of month/day is clear enough for transcription.
rows.english_description: Payment to Grove for bills relating to venison supplied on June 30th.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Grove for venison bills dated June 30th, amounting to £5 17s 0d.

rows.description: Allowed Mr Powell, Venison - Bills for repairs viz
rows.notes: This is a grouping/header line introducing several sub-bills; no amount appears on this descriptive line itself. 'Powell' and 'venison' are legible; subsequent lines contain numeric details.
rows.english_description: Allowance made to Mr Powell covering venison and associated repair bills; detailed below.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Allowance made to Mr Powell for venison and for repair bills (details follow); amount not stated on this line.

rows.notes: This sub-item under Mr Powell shows 2 4 0 in the currency columns. The descriptive word looks like 'Res' or similar abbreviation; transcription reflects the visible letters but meaning is unclear.
rows.english_description: Payment described as 'Res' (uncertain abbreviation) as part of the bills allowed to Mr Powell for repairs.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment labelled 'Res' (uncertain meaning) relating to Mr Powell's repair bills, amounting to £2 4s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 6 16 4 clearly aligned. The surname 'Hayward' is legible in the manuscript as the payee for this sub-bill.
rows.english_description: Payment to Hayward, likely for repair work (itemised under Mr Powell's allowed bills).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Hayward for repair-related work, amounting to £6 16s 4d.

rows.notes: Amount shown under shillings/pence as 17 2; no pounds written (recorded as 0). Name 'Garlick' clearly written.
rows.english_description: Payment to Garlick, likely a tradesman, for work or bills itemised under Mr Powell's account.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Garlick related to repair bills, amounting to £0 17s 2d.

rows.notes: Subtotal for Mr Powell's grouped repairs is clearly written as 9 17 6 aligned to the right. No explicit text on same line; grouping indicates this is a total.

rows.description: Grandham - repairing roof of Kidlington Chancel
rows.notes: Amount 2 6 0 clear. 'Grandham' (or 'Grandham') and the description 'repairing roof of Kidlington Chancel' read reasonably well though some letters are faded.
rows.english_description: Payment to Grandham for repairs to the roof of the chancel at Kidlington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Grandham for repairing the roof of the Kidlington chancel, amounting to £2 6s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 2 5 0 visible. The first word could be 'Tea' or 'Tear' (manuscript ambiguous); 'C. Fisher' is legible as recipient. Amount alignment is clear.
rows.english_description: Purchase of a tea coat (clothing) for C. Fisher.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Purchase of a tea coat for C. Fisher, amounting to £2 5s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 12 10 0 is readable and well aligned. The name and rest of the description are very faint/unclear in the manuscript; I have marked the uncertain portion as [illegible].
rows.english_description: Provisional stipend associated with Mr Pupart paid to an illegible/unspecified recipient.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Provisional stipend associated with Mr Pupart paid to an illegible/unspecified recipient, amounting to £12 10s 0d.

rows.description: Mr Hastings - Bills for repairs at Campsfield
rows.notes: Large amount 124 7 4 clearly shown and aligned. 'Mr Hastings' and 'Campsfield' readable; the hundred-and-twenty-four pounds figure is unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Repair bills for work at Campsfield paid to Mr Hastings.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Repair bills for work at Campsfield paid to Mr Hastings, amounting to £124 7s 4d.

rows.notes: Amount 30 5 9 clearly visible. Description reads as 'Halfyears Poor & Income Tax' (manuscript uses a contraction for 'Halfyears'); transcription follows visible wording.
rows.english_description: Half-year payment of the poor rate and income tax.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year payment of the poor rate and income tax, amounting to £30 5s 9d.

rows.notes: Final boxed total at the foot of the page reads L. 398 16 3 with 'Carried forward' written above it. Amount and label are clearly legible and aligned.
